name: All-Day-Long Crockpot Beef
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Jo Anne Merrill
Preparation Time: 8:00
Use any cut of beef roast desired.
1. Cut the beef roast into serving-sized portions. If more than 6 servings are needed, use additional meat. Brown the beef in a bit of vegetable oil, or skip this step if time is limited.
2. Slice the onion and separate it into rings. Dice the peeled carrots, celery, and peppers into thin strips or circles. Place these vegetables at the bottom of the crockpot. Sprinkle the beef pieces with freshly ground black pepper, minced garlic, and onion soup mix. Place on top of the vegetables.
3. Mix steak sauce and Worcestershire sauce in a small bowl with about 1/2 cup water and 1/2 cup tomato juice. Pour this mixture over the meat.
4. Turn the crockpot to high for 30 minutes, or skip this step if you're short on time. Then turn to low, cover, and cook for 7-9 hours. Keep the crockpot covered as much as possible throughout cooking.
A crockpot can be used to cook this meal for a varied amount of time, depending on your schedule. It is not necessary to remove the meat and vegetables at a specific point in time. One or two hours either way usually makes no difference.
When ready to serve, use a slotted spoon to dip the meat and vegetables out of the pot. The liquid can be used as is, or turn the crockpot to high and thicken the liquid with a little flour or cornstarch.
